title: Virginia bans sale of precise geolocation data
titleZh: 弗吉尼亚州禁止出售精确地理位置数据
author: toomuchtodo
relevanceReason: 命中 1 个 AI/科技关键词。
summaryRaw: On April 13, 2026, Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger signed into law S.B. 388, which amends the Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act (“VCDPA”) to prohibit the sale of geolocation data. Notably, the VCDPA defines “sale” more narrowly than other state comprehensive privacy laws, as “the exchange of personal data for monetary consideration by the controller to a third party.” The ban on the sale of geolocation data goes into effect on July 1, 2026. Virginia follows Maryland and Oregon in banning the sale of geolocation data. Both Maryland and Oregon more broadly define “sale” to mean the exchange of personal data “for monetary or other valuable consideration.” Virginia joins several other states that have recently proposed legislation with similar bans, including California, Massachusetts, Vermont and Washington State. The legislative activity follows regulatory scrutiny on the sale of geolocation data, including the California Attorney General’s investigation into the location data industry in March 2025, and a 2024 FTC settlement banning a data broker from selling geolocation data.
summaryZh: On April 13, 2026, Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger signed into law S.B. 388, which amends the Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act (“VCDPA”) to prohibit the sale of geolocation data. Notably, the VCDPA defines “sale” more narrowly than other state comprehensive privacy laws, as “the exchange of personal data for monetary consideration by the controller to a third party.” The ban on the sale of geolocation data goes into effect on July 1, 2026. Virginia follows Maryland and Oregon in banning the sale of geolocation data. Both Maryland and Oregon more broadly define “sale” to mean the exchange of personal data “for monetary or other valuable consideration.” Virginia joins several other states that have recently proposed legislation with similar bans, including California, Massachusetts, Vermont and Washington State. The legislative activity follows regulatory scrutiny on the sale of geolocation data, including the California Attorney General’s investigation into the location data industry in March 2025, and a 2024 FTC settlement banning a data broker from selling geolocation data.

comments.author: dv_dt
comments.textRawHtml: So let's say a Delaware incorporated company sells location data that happens to be collected in Virginia, but its sold from the corporation with no operations in Virginia. What happens?  On the other hand us-east-1 is in Virginia with who knows how many payment processing servers running.
comments.textZhHtml: 假设一家在特拉华州注册的公司出售碰巧在弗吉尼亚州收集的位置数据，但该公司在弗吉尼亚州没有任何业务运营。会发生什么？另一方面，us-east-1区域位于弗吉尼亚州，那里运行着不知多少台支付处理服务器。

children.author: b40d-48b2-979e
children.textRawHtml: The same thing that happens in court cases across state lines.  It goes to one of the jurisdictions in a lawsuit unless it qualifies for diversity jurisdiction and goes to a federal court.
children.textZhHtml: 和跨州诉讼案件的情况一样。它会进入诉讼中的某个管辖法院，除非它符合异籍管辖权条件并移交联邦法院。

children.author: piker
children.textRawHtml: Okay I tend to agree with the CivPro analysis here, but the parent was probably more concerned with the result on the merits.
children.textZhHtml: 好吧，我倾向于同意这里关于民事诉讼法的分析，但原帖作者可能更关心的是实体审理的结果。

children.author: spiralpolitik
children.textRawHtml:
If the Delaware company has zero presence in Virginia then the state of Virginia can't do anything about it.

The presence of us-east-1 in Virginia probably will complicate the matter considerably and I'm guessing it's something the courts would need to sort out.
children.textZhHtml:
如果这家特拉华州公司在弗吉尼亚州没有任何业务存在，那么弗吉尼亚州对此也无能为力。

us-east-1区域位于弗吉尼亚州可能会使事情变得相当复杂，我猜这需要法院来理清。

children.author: 8note
children.textRawHtml: whoever collected it in virginia, selling it to the delaware company would be screwed though?
children.textZhHtml: 无论谁在弗吉尼亚州收集它，然后将其卖给特拉华州的公司，都会被搞砸吗？

children.author: bitmasher9
children.textRawHtml: Yeah, someone is at risk of trafficking the gps data out of Virginia initially.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，有人最初面临着将 GPS 数据贩运出弗吉尼亚州的风险。

children.author: thephyber
children.textRawHtml: Most likely the vendor will make a judgement call about whether they care to comply. If they do want to comply, they will likely exempt all Virginia data from the collected data set and contractually require and downstream user to indemnify them if a Virginia person is affected by their data set.
children.textZhHtml: 最有可能的情况是，供应商会自行判断是否愿意遵守。如果他们确实想遵守，他们可能会将收集到的数据集中所有弗吉尼亚州的数据排除在外，并在合同中要求任何下游用户，如果其数据集影响到弗吉尼亚州居民，需对他们进行赔偿。

children.author: HoldOnAMinute
children.textRawHtml:
So, they are collecting geolocation data to avoid selling geolocation data.

BRILLIANT
children.textZhHtml:
因此，他们正在收集地理位置数据以避免出售地理位置数据。

太棒了

children.author: 8note
children.textRawHtml:
from the Youtube kids business, its pretty clear that even when they pretend to not know the details of whos being targeted, they still do, and sell targeted data as such

soo, might as well have the proper details be collected and not sellable, than collected/inferred and still sellable
children.textZhHtml:
从 Youtube 儿童业务来看，很明显，即使他们假装不知道谁成为目标的详细信息，他们仍然知道，并出售这样的目标数据

所以，与其收集/推断但仍然可以出售，还不如收集正确的详细信息而不是出售

comments.author: ascotan
comments.textRawHtml: The article is misleading. The sale of geolocation data can still take place but not for precise locations. The bill prevents the sale of data that can identify you within 1750ft. You can still be tracked just not precisely. i.e. companies will just sell fuzzy geolocation data.
comments.textZhHtml: 这篇文章具有误导性。地理位置数据的销售仍然可以进行，但不能是精确位置。该法案禁止出售能在1750英尺范围内识别你身份的数据。你仍然会被追踪，只是不够精确。也就是说，公司只会出售模糊的地理位置数据。

children.author: datahack
children.textRawHtml:
So, the fact that you went to a medical center just not the specific doctors office?

This seems weak.
children.textZhHtml:
所以，只是知道你去了一个医疗中心，而不是具体的医生办公室？

这似乎有点牵强。

children.author: abustamam
children.textRawHtml: Yeesh. Collect enough fuzzy data and you can identify a lot about someone.
children.textZhHtml: 天哪。收集足够多的模糊数据，你就能识别出关于某人的大量信息。

children.author: seanhunter
children.textRawHtml: Yes. This general problem is known as "k-anonymity". It's worth everyone who works with any sort of personally-identifiable data to read the original paper because the framework they identified is still really helpful for thinking about these issues. https://dataprivacylab.org/dataprivacy/projects/kanonymity/p...
children.textZhHtml: 是的。 这个普遍问题被称为“k-匿名”。每个使用任何类型的个人身份数据的人都值得阅读原始论文，因为他们确定的框架对于思考这些问题仍然非常有帮助。 https://dataprivacylab.org/dataprivacy/projects/kanonymity/p...

children.author: titzer
children.textRawHtml: 8 hours in fuzzy spot one, 8 hours in fuzzy spot two, fitting some gaussian distribution, and then a few points at other times, and boom, not anonymous at all.
children.textZhHtml: 在模糊点一处 8 小时，在模糊点二处 8 小时，拟合一些高斯分布，然后在其他时间有几个点，然后繁荣起来，根本不是匿名的。

children.author: baq
children.textRawHtml: add Cauchy noise, problem solved, right? …right?
children.textZhHtml: 加上柯西噪声，问题就解决了，对吧？……对吧？

children.author: 6LLvveMx2koXfwn
children.textRawHtml: Indeed, I am pretty sure an algorithm exists which both complies with the law and decompiles fuzzy data to 'less fuzzy' data, i.e. identifying data, thus obviating the ban.
children.textZhHtml: 事实上，我非常确定存在一种算法，既符合法律规定，又可以将模糊数据反编译为“不太模糊”的数据，即识别数据，从而避免禁令。

children.author: somenameforme
children.textRawHtml: Yeah I don't understand how something like this makes any sense. Just average out the data points and you're going to get pretty much a straight line path of where and what somebody did. Maybe if there's like one data point per week per person, but even that's going to be pretty informative when averaged as to exactly where somebody lives, works, and so on.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，我不明白这样的事情有什么意义。只需对数据点进行平均，您就可以得到某人在哪里以及做了什么的几乎一条直线路径。也许如果每个人每周有一个数据点，但即使是对于某人生活、工作的确切地点进行平均计算，也将提供相当多的信息。

children.author: gwerbin
children.textRawHtml: It's a classic legislation move to look like you're doing something in order to shut down public displeasure, while not actually doing anything.
children.textZhHtml: 这是典型的立法手段，表面上做点样子来平息公众的不满，实际上却什么也没干。

children.author: ezwoodland
children.textRawHtml: Depends on if the independent events are uncorrelated. For example, if they had to be quantized to a grid of length 1750ft and quantized sufficiently in time as well, it might be impossible to average out since the various data points provide no new information.
children.textZhHtml: 这取决于独立事件是否不相关。例如，如果必须将它们量化到长度为1750英尺的网格上，并且在时间上也进行充分量化，那么可能就无法求平均了，因为各个数据点没有提供新的信息。

children.author: seanhunter
children.textRawHtml:
This is why I posted the k-anonymity paper. A dataset is k-anonymous if using the dataset is is not possible to distinguish a given person in the dataset from k other people. Then we set a value of k we feel comfortable with and from that we can back into the areas which intuitively you would think would need to vary in size based on time of day and how many people travel through each area in the dataset.

It’s a lot of work but this is what you need to do to guarantee a given (non-zero) level of privacy. It can be done if people are serious about it.
children.textZhHtml:
这就是为什么我发布了k-匿名论文。如果一个数据集是k-匿名的，那么使用该数据集就无法将数据集中的某个特定个体与其他k个人区分开来。然后我们设定一个让我们觉得安心的k值，由此我们可以反推出各个区域，直觉上你会认为这些区域的大小需要根据一天中的不同时段以及数据集中经过每个区域的人数而变化。

这需要大量工作，但这就是保证一定（非零）隐私级别所必须做的。如果人们认真对待，这是可以做到的。
